zotero.abstractNote: Many unsupervised learning methods, such as normalizing flows and independent component analysis, require the computation of the Jacobian determinant of a transformation. This is computationally expensive, typically scaling as O(dˆ3) where d is the dimension of the data. In this paper, we propose a method to optimize the Jacobian term using relative gradients. This approach allows us to compute the gradient of the log-determinant of the Jacobian in O(dˆ2) time, which is a significant improvement over the standard O(dˆ3) approach. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on several unsupervised learning tasks, including density estimation and independent component analysis.
